revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:An (Shinto)]][[fr:An (shinto)]][[te:యన్ (షింటో)]]
[[Image:An_Shinto.jpg|300px|thumb|A ''haCategoryyaku-an'' carrying a ''[[tamagushi]]'']]
{{Shinto|qq=}}
The {{nihongo|'''''An'''''||qq=}} is a small {{ill|Table (furniture)|en|Table (furniture)|qid=Q14748|lt=table|1=Table (furniture)}}, desk or platform used during [[Shinto]] ceremonies to bear offerings. It may have four, eight or sixteen legs; the eight-legged variety, called {{nihongo|''hassoku-an'' or ''haCategoryyaku-an''|八足案, 八脚案||lit. "eight-legged table"|qq=}}, is the most common. {{cn|date= June 2022|qq=}}
== See also ==
* [[Glossary of Shinto]], for an explanation of terms concerning Shinto, Shinto art, and Shinto shrine architecture.
* ''Basic Terms of Shinto'', Kokugakuin University, Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics, Tokyo 1985
==References==
* {{cite encyclopedia|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=300|encyclopedia=Encyclopedia of Shinto|publisher=Institute of Japanese Culture and Classics, Kokugakuin University|location=Tokyo, Japan|year=2006|access-date=2007-08-07|title=An|qq=}}
* {{cite book|last=Picken|first=Stuart|title=Essentials of Shinto: An Analytical Guide to Principal Teachings|url=https://archive.org/details/essentialsshinto00pick|url-access=limited|publisher=Greenwood Publishing Group|pages=[https://archive.org/details/essentialsshinto00pick/page/n403 369]|year=1994|isbn=0313264317|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{Shinto shrine|qq=}}



[[wiktionary:案|案]]
